text.title: Data Analysis — Requirements Discovery and Quality Assessment Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the data-analysis methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (identify data needs): List the data elements the answer would require; reject decorative requests.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the identify data needs gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (define data elements): Per-element row: name, type, source, scope, owner.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the define data elements gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (build data model): Conceptual → logical → physical ER snapshot; tag bounded contexts.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the build data model gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (assess quality): Score 6 dimensions (accuracy, completeness, consistency, timeliness, validity, uniqueness); flag below threshold.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the assess quality gate.

rule.statement: Step 5 (extract rules): Emit validation/derivation/default/constraint/uniqueness rules linked to BR-IDs.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the extract rules gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions for data-analysis do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
